name: Information Quality
definition: Information Quality assesses the **quality, accuracy, and completeness of the generated clinical note** — the output artifact of the ambient AI system. It asks: is the note good enough to use?
keyFinding: Across all 5 simulation studies in the corpus (Arko 2025, Biro 2025, Anderson 2025, Foo 2025, Kernberg 2023), omissions are the dominant error type (71–86% of all errors). Hallucinations/additions are secondary. More clinically dangerous than hallucinations at scale because missing information is ha
belongsHere: Note completeness (are all clinically relevant elements captured?)
Note accuracy / factual correctness (hallucination rate, incorrect facts)
Overall note quality scores (PDQI-9, PDSQI-9)
Note length and verbosity (as proxy for thoroughness vs. over-generation)
Automated text similarity metrics (ROUGE, BERTScore) — methodological
Coding accuracy (ICD-10, HCC capture) — insofar as it reflects documentation completeness

name: Net Benefits
definition: Net Benefits captures the **downstream, tangible outcomes** generated by using the ambient AI system. It asks: what concrete value did this produce — for clinicians, patients, and the organization?
belongsHere: Documentation time (objective, clock-measured)
After-hours documentation (work outside work)
Chart closure timeliness
Total EHR time
Patient experience and relationship quality
Physician-patient interaction quality (face time, attention)
Clinical patient safety outcomes
Financial productivity (RVU / revenue impact)
Coding accuracy and completeness (ICD-10, HCC, E/M)
Patient volume and throughput

name: System Quality
definition: System Quality assesses the **technical performance of the ambient AI tool itself** — upstream of the output note. It asks: does the machine work reliably, accurately, and consistently?
keyFinding: SQ is the most evidence-sparse dimension in the corpus. Commercial ambient AI products (DAX, Abridge, Suki, Ambience) do not publish transcription accuracy, WER, or diarization performance data — a documented pattern of industry opacity since van Buchem 2021. Only 3 papers formally measure ASR accur
belongsHere: Transcription accuracy (Word Error Rate, Medical Term Recall, Speaker Diarization)
System reliability, uptime, latency
Benchmark dataset availability and reproducibility
Specialty/domain coverage of the evaluation pipeline
LLM-as-evaluator correlation with human raters
Study design rigor and methodological validity

name: SerQ — Service Quality
definition: Service Quality assesses the **vendor, implementation, and support experience** — the organizational and operational wrapper around the ambient AI tool. It asks: was the deployment and ongoing support effective?
keyFinding: SerQ is the most underrepresented dimension in peer-reviewed literature — only 8 papers address it formally. Most implementation experience is captured through qualitative methods (interviews, thematic analysis) rather than validated instruments. The FIM/AIM/IUS scales appear in Suhail 2026. PHTI 20
belongsHere: Implementation barriers and workflow fit
Onboarding and training quality
EHR integration experience (FHIR, APIs, technical support)
Learning curve and workflow adaptation
Specialty fit and template compatibility
Governance model and patient consent compliance
Adoption barriers related to structural/support factors

name: Use / Intention to Use
definition: Use captures **whether and how much the ambient AI tool is actually used** — both observed behavior (actual utilization) and expressed intention to continue using it. It asks: are people adopting it, at what intensity, and will they keep using it?
keyFinding: Every large deployment study finds heterogeneous adoption: heavy users, moderate users, and low/non-users. Dose-response is consistent — heavy users benefit most (Pearlman 2025, Omon 2025, Lukac 2025). Tierney 2025 (2.5M uses): top-third of users account for disproportionate % of total use. Tierney
belongsHere: Utilization rate (% of encounters using ambient AI)
Total sessions / volume of use over time
Adoption stratification (heavy vs. light vs. non-users)
Post-trial adoption (revealed preference)
Intention to use / willingness to adopt
Long-term use intent and recommendation intention
User adoption predictors (what characteristics predict use)

name: User Satisfaction
definition: User Satisfaction captures **how clinicians subjectively experience the ambient AI tool** — their emotional states, well-being, perceived usability, and trust. It asks: do people feel better for having used it, and do they find it trustworthy and useful?
keyFinding: US is the most commonly measured dimension (35 papers) but is subject to substantial selection bias — most studies recruit interested volunteers. The RCT (Afshar 2025b) shows burnout reduces (−0.44 PFI, p<0.001) but professional fulfillment does not significantly increase (p=0.04 vs pre-specified 0.
belongsHere: Clinician burnout and work exhaustion (validated scales: PFI, Mini-
